desc:
Prickly shrubs and thorny bushes pack what little spaces there are between the
majestically tall trees. Dark, flying shapes flit from branch to branch overhead
and the ever-present insect buzzing seems a bit louder in this place. Unseen
creatures rustle within the deep thickets as their home is disturbed.
name: Dense Thicket

desc:
The area is surrounded on all sides by dark foliage which makes travelling
difficult and limits visibility to minimum. The trees stand tall, reaching so
high that their tops are not visible in the deep darkness. There is a straight
row of mounds on the ground, next to something that was once a man-made
structure: now only the overgrown foundation gives hint to its former existence.
name: Deep Glade

desc:
An unusual haze has settled upon this part of Mirkwood. Though not quite at the
level of fog, this mist is thick enough to reduce visibility in all directions.
The filtering effect gives the forest shapes a grotesque quality and suggests
movement just outside of reach.
name: Misty Mirkwood

desc:
The normal forest sounds lessen in this deep and dark portion of Mirkwood.
Spider webs have been woven between the black trunks, protruding branches and
hanging vines. So dense is the vegetation that only the quiet crunch of steps on
dried leaves comes through. The air feels close and tight, making it more
difficult to breathe.
name: Deep, Dark Forest

desc:
Massive, silken cobwebs intertwined with the skeletal canopies of several trees
form this platform sixteen feet up in the air. The space is fairly confined,
perhaps six feet by six. A webbed tunnel leads north from here, and passage is
also possible down to the forest floor.
name: Web-infested Platform

desc:
Heavy, sticky cobwebs tightly woven together with debris and boughs form this
almost bridge-like gangway between two tree crowns. The sky is completely
blocked out by the canopy above, a leafy, webbed entity that rustles ceaselessly
as if animated from within by something fairly large. The passageway spirals
lazily upwards upon a sticky platform, following the natural shapes of the dark
forest.
name: Webbed Gangway

desc:
Movement in this narrow space is severely hampered by the sharp, thorny branches
that seem to grasp at every movement. The branches themselves are covered in
sticky, slimy cobwebs in varying stages of decay. The smell in here is
unbelievable and can only be described as utterly alien yet frightfully
familiar: akin to something sickly-sweet that has been burned by some
unthinkable otherworldly flame. Passage is possible down, north and west,
further into similar webbed, confined areas.
name: Slimy Platform

desc:
It is hard to get a general idea of the layout of this webbed space, where
strands upon strands of silky filaments hang from bough to bough, creating a
dark, confined space where twisting passages lead further into this
acrid-smelling maze. To the north the cobwebs appear to have been torn apart by
something forceful and sharp.
name: Confusion of Webs

desc:
This tunnel bends around a trunk, or perhaps it is an exceptionally thick bough.
No longer a neat circle in shape, the tunnel is squashed by the bend. Intricate
and beautiful, the webs here are pristine, pure white silk entwining slivers of
silver. In the south it appears major damage has been done to the tunnel;
something has sliced through web and leaf alike.
name: Sticky Passage

desc:
The thick foliage of the trees above prevents the sun ridding the forest floor
of its perpetual dusk. These woods seem almost too thorny to allow travelling in
any direction, though one might try to squeeze through the dense thickets
growing in the east and south. Going northwards seems impossible, while
westwards it appears to be clearer.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
The canopy of treetops overhead block out direct light, adding to the feeling of
tension that underlies all of Mirkwood. Red and verdant mosses tangle with
thick, tawny vines to create a densely packed forest. Moist, decaying leaves
exude a sour smell when disturbed. Beetles and ants seem to crawl everywhere.
name: Thick Tangle in Mirkwood

desc:
Dark, tall trees stand silently in the forest, spreading their branches in all
directions and intermingling with each other to create an intact canopy which
blocks all the light to reach the ground. The air is thick and heavy with a
distinct odour of moist and rotting plants rising from the dank undergrowth.
Undisturbed fungi and lichens choke the surrounding tree trunks.
name: Shadowy Forest

desc:
Thick and humid air weaves in and around the stout trunks and hanging vines. The
moisture in the air encourages the lichens to bloom on almost every stationary
surface. Slimy moss and vines dangle from overhead branches to form what feels
like wet hair that has to be pushed through in order to pass.
name: Humid Forest

desc:
Thick boughs and a dense canopy of leaves form a roof above that blots out the
sky completely, not allowing even a sliver of light through. Yet even in the
perpetual gloom here on the forest floor, dense tangles of undergrowth seem to
thrive. A scent of corruption drifts through the forest, seemingly emanating
from grey, pallid mushrooms that grow in clusters on some of the ancient
tree-trunks.
name: Gloomy Woodlands

desc:
This gloomy part of Mirkwood is overgrown with large tangles of intertwining
brush and bramble. Old, twisted trees rise up from among the brambles, the
heavily-leaved treetops forming a dense canopy above. The only viable directions
of travel are to the south, deeper into the tangles, or up a short slope towards
the east.
name: In the Tangle

desc:
Large, tangled shrubs of evergreen flourish in this part of Mirkwood forest. The
dense branches above blot out most of the sky, yet at places, openings in the
canopy allow a glimpse of the heavens above. The light that might slip through
is not enough to lift the deep shadows that shroud the forest.
name: Abundant Undergrowth

desc:
Great thickets of thorny, entangled shrubs cover the forest floor, their
tangled, twisted branches intertwining so much that it is all but impossible to
tell where one large bush ends and the one next to it begins. Yet even amongst
this abundant undergrowth, there seems to be just enough open spaces to squeeze
through to other areas of the forest, if one do not mind being pricked and stung
by the many thorns.
name: Deep in Abundant Undergrowth

desc:
Thick, slithering vines crawl up the suffering trunks of ancient oaks. The vines
ensnare the ancient, gnarled trunks as if to choke the life out of them. Large
patches of bark have come off the trees and the wood underneath is covered by a
thick, slimy layer of grey mould. The many large, tangled bushes growing here
collude to make these woods a hazardous maze to travel, with sharp thorns ready
to sting and snag, and twisted branches and knotted roots to trip and hinder,
the careless traveller.
name: Abundant Undergrowth

desc:
This part of the forest is almost cave-like in appearance. The vegetation is
thick and impassable on all sides except a small opening between two ancient,
gnarled oaks to the north. The centre of this small hollow is surprisingly free
of undergrowth, creating a small open area maybe twenty feet across. Yet even
there long branches reach out above, blotting out the sky and shrouding the
small glade in darkness.
name: A Shrouded Glade

desc:
A variety of bushes and shrubs thrive here, despite the shade and gloom. Little
light manages to find its way down through the dense crowns of the trees. Large
mushroom patches grow on the ground, but many of them seem to be attacked by a
parasite or suffer from some kind of disease for they are covered with specks of
grey mould and surrounded by a thick stench of rot.
name: Abundant Undergrowth

desc:
The ground is wet and soggy, almost marsh-like. Ancient stands of beech and
maple intermingle with tangled growths of thorn-covered bushes. The air is thick
with the smell of decay and the ground is covered with a moist mess of
decomposing plants and leaves.
name: Soggy Tangles

desc:
Small, filthy pools of shallow water filled with rotting vegetation have formed
between the trees. The murky gloom and tangled undergrowth make it all but
impossible to separate solid ground from rotten plants with water underneath,
yet with care it might be possible to move from one dry spot to another.
name: Deep in the Black Morass

desc:
Great, old trees with their gnarled trunks, hanging boughs and intact crowns
grow in abundance in this area, preventing sunlight from reaching the ever-dark
ground of Mirkwood. Between the trunks enormous, tangled bushes have conquered
whatever space is left. The ground itself is covered with black leaves and other
litter.
name: Tangled Forest

desc:
A tight, endless canopy darkens the forest. Below it, the ground slopes slightly
upwards to the east where a continuous mesh of sharp-leafed, black bushes are
growing next to each other, completely filling the ground and blocking travel in
that direction. The bushes have encircled the gigantic tree trunks, and
intermingled with the vines hanging from the dark, thick boughs.
name: Tangled Forest

desc:
Some sturdy branches of dark oaks are growing near to the ground, allowing
travel in the lower canopy of Mirkwood. Down below, the ground is completely
choked by the dense, intermingled undergrowth. Giant roots are stretching
outwards, thorny bushes grow all around, and sticky vines complete the chaos by
binding shrubs and boughs together.
name: Unstable Ground-level Boughs

desc:
The oppressive, dark forest continues in all directions. Gloomy black alders
tower upwards and their wide, black crowns integrate into each other high above,
creating an intact canopy which blocks out all the light from the sky. The
ground slopes steeply towards the east. The numerous roots and darkling shrubs
that cover it, seem to shift around in the darkness, as if blocking the way on
purpose.
name: Tangled Forest

desc:
A narrow, forested ravine runs between two slopes, which tower upwards to the
west and east like darkened, tree-covered walls. At the bottom of the hollow the
air is moist, making the black, dense undergrowth wet and partly rotten. Strong
roots twist on the ground, black leaves create a sticky layer of forest litter,
and ferns grow here in abundance.
name: Bottom of a Tree-covered Slope

desc:
The air is humid in this part of Mirkwood. Giant, poisonous-looking ferns cover
the forest floor and among them occasional, moss-covered rocks litter the
ground. The black oaks and other trees are infested by a sickness, as some of
the branches are leafless and dead, and on the trunks of the giant trees are
grow sickly-green fungi.
name: Tangled Forest

desc:
The ground here is soft and wet. Small, shallow pools of dark, vile water have
gathered in the lower parts of hollows. Dense undergrowth grows across drier
land and foul water alike, leaving few paths open for travel. Amidst the
undergrowth, gnarled and twisted trunks of oak and beech rise up, their heavy
boughs above casting the forest into gloomy shadows.
name: In the Tangle

desc:
Vast expanses of densely growing shrubs and brush fill every hollow and nook,
making passage difficult as sharp thorns snag, prickle and sting even the most
heedful traveller. The dark shadows and perpetual gloom further heighten the
need for caution when attempting to traverse these woods.
name: Gloomy Woodlands

desc:
The dreary, gloomy forest is filled with an almost tangible smell of rot and
decay. Slithering lengths of mottled vines creep up the old, gnarled trunks
amidst clusters of parasitic fungi. Thick, knotted roots crawling across the
ground and thickets of thorn-covered bushes collaborate to make these woods very
difficult to travel through.
name: Amidst the Gloomy Tangles

desc:
Great, sprawling shadows of huge, tangled bushes block passage towards the
south, but in other directions it might be possible to squeeze through the dense
undergrowth. The forest lies deep in shadows, the dense canopy above shrouding
the sky from view and it is hard to descry any landmarks to orient by.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The Forest of Mirkwood completely encloses this space with a thick layer of
branches overhead and bristling, thorny vines and tall bushes interspersed with
dark, stout tree trunks on every side. Glistening, ebony tendrils dangle from
above, while obisidian weeds and grasses provide an odd carpet underfoot.
name: Black Forest

desc:
Twisted, gnarled trunks of oak cast strange shadows in the gloom, darker shades
of black against a forest floor that has not seen sunlight for centuries. There
are no distinguishing landmarks here, nothing to differentiate one part of the
area from the others around it. Without a proper sense of direction, it would be
possible to wander this dark forest for eternity without finding a way out.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Mirkwood opens up into a space where no vegetation grows. Instead, the remnants
of tree trunks and undergrowth are flattened in a pattern resembling a
concentric circle. In the middle of the circle, nothing grows or lives. It is
obvious some spell or power was used here, destroying all of the surroundings.
name: Barren, Blasted Forest

desc:
A strong and wide spider web rises almost vertically along a huge oak trunk
here. The threads of the web spread from its centre in concentric circles,
creating an unusual but climbable passageway between different layers of the
shadowy canopy. Some half-rotten bird corpses hang from the web: victims of the
spiders who hunt here.
name: Vertical Wall of Spider Web

desc:
The tunnel stops at a dead-end, the only way out from here being to the east.
The walls, floor and ceiling are all covered with several layers of sticky,
veil-like spider web which lend to this dark, gruesome area shades of grey and
white. Some cocoons are hanging in mid-air.
name: Surrounded by Thick, Sticky Webs

desc:
There is a vast, level surface inside the canopy. Some thick branches form its
foundation, which has been strengthened with vegetation and spider silk - a
seemingly endless amount of sticky, grey spider silk. The platform continues to
the north and shows signs of frequent usage: at places it has been worn almost
smooth. A giant spider web has been woven at its edges, starting here and
continuing downwards into the darkness, creating a deceptive trap.
name: Webbed Platform

desc:
A platform made of vines, twigs and spider silk follows the shapes of the
supporting branches, altering its way to the left and right. A dense, fence-like
tangle of spider web encircles sticky cocoons hanging from the upper branches.
The cocoons are grey and of different sizes, but all hold their unlucky victims
securely fast. A strong stench of rotten meat pervades the area.
name: Grey, Stinking Cocoons

desc:
A cylindrical tunnel spirals around an old, web-infested trunk, leading to an
open platform at the top, and to a narrow, slimy passageway at the bottom. The
bark of the trunk is rather smooth, as if having been ground down by someone or
something for a long time. A continuous wall of spider web has been woven on
leafy branches, limiting visibility to no further than the next turn of the
tunnel.
name: Web-infested Branches

desc:
Some twisted, thorny branches impale the webbed walls of this narrow tunnel,
making passage somewhat difficult. There are red stains on the branches and on
the floor, and the walls are covered with trickles of stinking, grey slime,
acidic-looking and sticky to touch. The tunnel splits into three directions,
leading east, south and up.
name: Slimy, Sticky Tunnel

desc:
The foul-smelling, narrow tunnel makes its way inside the canopy. The branches
which serve as the floor of the tunnel are somewhat bumpy due to the numerous
fungi that infest them. The way gets narrower and pokier towards the east, and
finally ends at a massive, strong spider web which blocks the tunnel altogether.
The only way out of this trap is to the west.
name: Cramped Tunnel

desc:
This shaky, swinging platform within the canopy has been woven from spider silk
wrapped tightly around the branches. Although the threads have been pasted
together with a sticky, green slime and litter from the trees, the supporting
branches themselves are not strong enough to keep the landing firm and stable.
To the north, the platform gets a roof as it leads into a tunnel made of spider
webs. The web continues downwards, creating a ladder-like construction allowing
one to climb down from the canopy.
name: Webbed Platform

desc:
Long, broad branches reach out towards the darkness, sometimes meeting each
other, sometimes ending abruptly in the air. A natural gangway has formed upon
some of them, allowing movement from tree to tree. On some boughs grows a thick
layer of ancient lichen, while others are totally smooth and clean, hinting that
the passageway is in frequent use.
name: On a Gangway upon Branches

desc:
Mossy vines hang from the shadowy canopy, entangling themselves with the gnarly
branches that stick out from a giant black elm tree. The surrounding canopy is
very dense but provides no proper foothold, so that moving from the side of the
trunk is not possible save to the west, onto the sturdy branches.
name: On the Branches of a Huge, Black Elm

desc:
A treeless circle approximately 50 yards from one end to another opens up under
the intact canopy of the surrounding trees. The black, gnarled trunks form a
wall all around, as if trying to conquer the open space. The process has already
begun, as sharp thorn-bushes grow in abundance in the grove. Any spaces there
might be between the trees have been filled with thick, sticky spider webs.
name: Circle under the Black Trees

desc:
A treeless circle approximately 50 yards from one end to another opens up under
the intact canopy of the surrounding trees. The black, gnarled trunks form a
wall all around, as if trying to conquer the open space. The process has already
begun, as sharp thorn-bushes grow in abundance in the grove. Any spaces there
might be between the trees have been filled with thick, sticky spider webs.
name: Circle under the Black Trees

desc:
A treeless circle approximately 50 yards from one end to another opens up under
the intact canopy of the surrounding trees. The black, gnarled trunks form a
wall all around, as if trying to conquer the open space. The process has already
begun, as sharp thorn-bushes grow in abundance in the grove. Any spaces there
might be between the trees have been filled with thick, sticky spider webs.
name: Circle under the Black Trees

desc:
A treeless circle approximately 50 yards from one end to another opens up under
the intact canopy of the surrounding trees. The black, gnarled trunks form a
wall all around, as if trying to conquer the open space. The process has already
begun, as sharp thorn-bushes grow in abundance in the grove. Any spaces there
might be between the trees have been filled with thick, sticky spider webs.
name: Circle under the Black Trees

desc:
This area has been conquered by poisonous-looking bushes. They grow all around
the place, between and around every single dark trunk that stands majestically
in the sea of vegetation. Some of the bushes have giant, red flowers which exude
an odd, stunning stench pervading the area, mixing with the other scents of the
forest. Sticky black ooze coats the uncountable leaves.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
The passageway through the forest is more like a dark tunnel inside the earth
than an actual passageway. Giant, moss-covered trunks lean greedily forward, as
if trying to grab any travellers. Their twisting branches and squirming roots
hinder movement, and render leaving the tunnel impossible. Visibility is limited
to few dozen yards at best.
name: Thick Forest

desc:
The ground of this area is sloping slowly but steadily downwards to the west.
Tall, pointy beeches grow on the slope, their moss-covered, black trunks curving
lazily over the area forming an arched roof above. Dark vines are hanging from
the canopy. There is a massive skeleton of some four-legged mammal on the
ground, its pale bones creating a sharp contrast to the black vegetation of the
forest.
name: Thick Forest

desc:
The canopy of Mirkwood hangs very low here, preventing the daylight from shining
upon this place, ever. Entangled, impenetrable-looking brush has conquered the
ground, which slopes steeply to the west. The vegetation leaves only a few
narrow black gaps between the tree trunks and giant-sized shrubs through which
one can venture still deeper into Mirkwood. Gnarled roots twist along the
ground, embracing each rock, stump and other bulge in their firm grip.
name: Thick Forest

desc:
The ground is wet and marsh-like. Thick, knotted roots slither just beneath the
surface of the shallow, filthy water as if lying in wait to trip unwary
travellers. There is plenty of dry, solid ground interspersed with the shallow,
murky pools, yet this are mostly occupied with large, tangled brambles. Thick,
gnarled trunks of ancient trees can be seen all across the area and their thick
boughs above form a dense canopy that blots out the sky.
name: Entangled Swamplands

desc:
A huge spider web has been woven in the air, spanning at dizzying heights
between the boughs and branches. The further away one proceeds from the
diamond-like centre of this giant web, the thinner the sticky threads get. The
web does not contain any large entangled victims, and no cocoons are visible. On
the very border of the web, the small, silky threads are fragile as a single
drop of morning dew in the sun.
name: The Outer Threads of the Spider Web

desc:
A huge spider web has been woven in the air, spanning at dizzying heights
between the boughs and branches. The further away one proceeds from the
diamond-like centre of this giant web, the thinner the sticky threads get. The
web does not contain any large entangled victims, and no cocoons are visible. On
the very border of the web, the small, silky threads are fragile as a single
drop of morning dew in the sun.
name: The Outer Threads of the Spider Web

desc:
A huge spider web has been woven in the air, spanning at dizzying heights
between the boughs and branches. The further away one proceeds from the
diamond-like centre of this giant web, the thinner the sticky threads get. The
web does not contain any large entangled victims, and no cocoons are visible. On
the very border of the web, the small, silky threads are fragile as a single
drop of morning dew in the sun.
name: The Outer Threads of the Spider Web

desc:
A narrow, passable route snakes through the forest, curving round obstacles, in
general running from the north to the east. Trunks and dense, impenetrable brush
border the way, blocking any attempts at trespass. Above, the black branches
hang low, filled with cobwebs and rotten vines.
name: Thick Forest

desc:
A short, steep slope leads downwards to the south, deeper into the tangled
undergrowth. Here at the top of the slope, the undergrowth is not quite as dense
and tangled but thick enough to block passage towards the east and west. Thick,
dense branches above block out the sky from sight and the forest is shrouded in
shadows and gloom.
name: Above the Tangle

desc:
Wide expanses of thick, impassable brush encompass the gnarled old trees in this
part of Mirkwood forest. The dark, sprawling shapes of vast thickets reach
almost high enough to merge with the wide, heavily-leaved treetops that blot out
the sky above. The only obvious route out of these tangles lies towards the
west, where narrow gaps between thorny bushes might allow those who do not mind
being scratched and pricked to squeeze through.
name: In the Tangled Undergrowth

desc:
The forest forms a surprisingly straight and clear passageway that cuts through
the wilderness in north-south direction. Some weeds, smaller bushes and
occasional roots cover the ground, while dense shrubs of black willows grow
everywhere around, entangled together, reluctant to allow passage forward.
Beyond the willows, huge trees of Mirkwood stand in unison, covering everything
under the dark canopy.
name: Avenue of Black, Wild Willows

desc:
A twisting and narrow pathway exists between an exceptionally dense and bushy
cluster of black willow trees. The shadowy and slender bushes have grown around
the area, limiting the visibility to minimum, forcing the path to zig-zag back
and forth, and making it very hard to tell which way leads to which direction.
Grey spider webs have been woven to the branches.
name: Avenue of Black, Wild Willows

desc:
Thick, dense gatherings of brambles grow all through the forest, ensnaring the
moss-covered trunks of old, hoary oaks. The sempiternal gloom continues
endlessly in all directions, yet even so the thorny, tangled undergrowth seems
to compose an insurmountable obstruction for travel in any direction except
possibly towards the west, and down a short slope towards the east.
name: Tangled Brambles

desc:
The wet, spongy ground is shrouded in shadow and gloom. The decaying vegetation
lies gathered in shallow pools of dark, foul water. Mottled layers of mould and
clusters of sickly grey fungi grow among the rotting plants and there is a
powerful stench of decay in the air.
name: Shadowy Bog

desc:
Large, thorny brambles grow in all directions, severely limiting the choice of
directions to travel in. Visibility is further reduced by the deep shadows cast
by a dense canopy above. Towards the east and west, narrow gaps between large
shrubs might provide a thoroughfare to other parts of Mirkwood, while any
attempts to force passage to the north and south would probably prove futile.
name: In the Tangled Brambles

desc:
A massive tangle of brambles has spread throughout Mirkwood here, all but
completely filling every available space between the stands of beech and small,
dense groves of fir. The air is filled with a scent of corruption and the
brambles are overgrown by long strands of grey lichen that cover the undergrowth
like enormous spider webs. A short slope westwards leads deeper into the gloom.
name: Tangled Brambles
